title: Build Jan-Dhan Gateway Fraud-Proof Benefit Distribution System

requirements.text: Create backend data model for Jan-Dhan Registry with fields: Citizen_ID (12-digit text), Income_Tier, Scheme_Eligibility, Scheme_Amount (number), Last_Claim_Date (timestamp), Region_Code, Account_Status (Active/Inactive), Aadhaar_Linked (boolean), Claim_Count (number). Support CSV import of approximately 2000 records.

quotes: Citizen_ID	Income_Tier	Scheme_Eligibility	Scheme_Amount	Last_Claim_Date	Region_Code	Account_Status	Aadhaar_Linked	Claim_Count

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores all 9 fields from the registry schema
CSV import endpoint accepts and parses registry data
Citizen_ID is stored as text and preserved exactly as provided

requirements.text: Implement ID-Hash Validator that converts every 12-digit Citizen_ID into SHA-256 hash for processing. Store and compare hashes to detect duplicate claims and block replay attempts immediately.

quotes: Convert every 12-digit Citizen_ID into a secure SHA-256 hash
Detects duplicate claims via hash collision

acceptanceCriteria: Every transaction hashes the Citizen_ID using SHA-256
System maintains hash registry to prevent duplicate claims
Replay attempts are rejected before processing

requirements.text: Build immutable hash-linked ledger that records each approved transaction with: Timestamp, Citizen_ID_Hash, Scheme, Amount, Previous_Line_Hash, Current_Line_Hash. Current_Hash must equal SHA256(Timestamp + CitizenHash + Scheme + Amount + PreviousHash). After every transaction, compute full file integrity hash and freeze system on mismatch.

quotes: Each approved transaction must be appended to a text ledger
Current_Hash = SHA256(Timestamp + CitizenHash + Scheme + Amount + PreviousHash)

acceptanceCriteria: Ledger stores all six required fields for each transaction
Each entry's Current_Hash correctly chains to Previous_Line_Hash
System computes and verifies full ledger integrity after each write
Tampering detection triggers automatic system freeze

requirements.text: Implement Gate 1: Eligibility Gate validation that checks Citizen_ID exists in registry, Account_Status equals Active, Aadhaar_Linked equals TRUE, Scheme_Eligibility matches requested scheme, Scheme_Amount matches fixed scheme mapping, and Claim_Count is 3 or less (reject or flag for manual review if exceeded).
acceptanceCriteria: All six eligibility conditions are validated before proceeding
Transaction is rejected if any condition fails
Claim_Count exceeding 3 triggers rejection or manual review flag

requirements.text: Implement Gate 2: Budget Gate with initial budget of ₹10,00,000 INR. Deduct amount only after approval, reject transaction if insufficient balance, and auto-lock system when budget reaches zero.
acceptanceCriteria: Budget starts at exactly ₹10,00,000
Budget is only deducted after transaction approval
Transactions are rejected when budget is insufficient
System automatically locks when budget reaches zero

requirements.text: Implement Gate 3: Frequency Gate that blocks claims within 30 days of Last_Claim_Date. Reject transaction immediately if violation occurs.

quotes: No claims within 30 days of Last_Claim_Date

acceptanceCriteria: System calculates date difference between current date and Last_Claim_Date
Transactions are rejected if less than 30 days have passed
Last_Claim_Date is updated only after successful transaction

requirements.text: Implement automatic system lock mechanism that freezes all new transactions when: ledger tampering is detected, budget is exhausted, hash chain mismatch occurs, or admin manually pauses the system. Blocked transactions must show clear rejection reason.
acceptanceCriteria: System maintains active/paused/frozen status state
All four lock conditions are monitored and trigger freeze
New transactions are blocked when system is not active
Lock reason is captured and returned to user

requirements.text: Create admin kill-switch dashboard displaying: System Status Indicator (Active/Paused/Frozen), Emergency Pause Button, Budget Remaining Display, and Transaction Count Monitor. Emergency pause must immediately block all new transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows real-time system status
Emergency pause button immediately updates system state
Budget remaining displays current balance in INR
Transaction count shows total processed transactions
All controls are functional and update backend state

requirements.text: Build transaction processing interface where users can submit benefit claims by entering Citizen_ID and selecting scheme. Display validation results for all three gates, show approval/rejection status with detailed reason, and update transaction history in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: User can input Citizen_ID and select scheme from available options
Interface displays results from eligibility, budget, and frequency gates
Approval or rejection is clearly shown with specific reason
Transaction history updates immediately after processing

requirements.text: Create beautiful, professional frontend UI for both transaction processing and admin dashboard using modern design patterns. Interface must be intuitive, responsive, and clearly communicate system security features.
acceptanceCriteria: UI uses cohesive color scheme and typography
Layout is clean and professional
Security features are visually emphasized
Interface is responsive across screen sizes
User actions provide clear visual feedback

constraints: System must use SHA-256 hashing only (no other hash algorithms)
Budget must start at exactly ₹10,00,000 INR
Frequency gate must enforce strict 30-day blocking period
Ledger tampering detection must freeze entire system immediately
All validation gates must pass before any transaction approval
CSV dataset must contain approximately 2000 records with exact schema provided
nonGoals: Integration with actual government Aadhaar systems
